metadata.title:
SHORTER


LIGHT CONTRACT

CounclImen Would Limit W. W. P. Co.'S Next
cInch on City to One Year In Interests


Of Cheaper Service.

pages.raw_text:
The Washington Water Power
Co. last night notified the city coun-
Gil that its 5-year lighting contract
would expire August 81, and want
ed arrangements made for renewal
so that the additional arc lamps
needed could be ordered. The COM
was referred to the
committee of the whole.


The disposition of councilmen
seems to be to limit the next con.
tract to 1 year and in the meantime
investigate the proposition of a
lighting plant conducted
by the city. This question will be
discussed next Tuesday night If it
is decided to take up and consider
the power monopoly's communica-
ton.


A year ago Capt. John Gray faith
cred an ordinance to inaugurate
lighting, but the council
got nowhere with 1t, the time being
considered inopportune for a
change. According to Councilman
Phillips, conditions have changed
now and the time is ripe for the
city to embark in the business of
making it's own light and selling 1t
to consumers in competition with
the monopoly. He said: 81 have
always favored city lighting plant
and favor it now. got figures one
time which showed that a plant
could be installed for $4,000 that
would furnish light for city build
ing and street lamps, and also to
individuals. The w. w. P. Co. is
making thousands Of dollars clear
profit out of the city lighting alone,
and there is no reason that l can
see why plant should not prove
successful"


Councilman Laumer said: 81
highly favor such a proposition and
want to see the Washington Water
Power Co.'S next contract limited
to / year, so as to give us time to
consider the city light"
